evals.prompt: Review this PR diff (evals/files/sample.diff) as if it were the output of `git diff dev...HEAD`.
evals.expected_output: Catches the planted violations — direct apiClient call in a page, window.confirm(), inline style, array of any[], a new npm script not documented in the README — with file:line and an exact fix, using the report structure.
expectations: Flags the direct apiClient.GET()/apiClient.DELETE() calls inside the Billing page as belonging in a service function instead
Flags window.confirm() and says it should be an AlertDialog instead
Flags the inline style={{ marginTop: ... }} attribute
Flags the new export-report npm script as something the README's Other Scripts section should document
Uses the report structure (Summary / High / Medium / Low / Looks good) with severity icons
Ends by asking whether the user wants a deeper /code-review pass

evals.prompt: Review this PR diff (evals/files/sample-readme-only.diff) as if it were the output of `git diff dev...HEAD`.
evals.expected_output: Correctly reports few or no findings for a README-only change rather than inventing issues to fill out every category.
expectations: Reports 'None' (or equivalent) for High and Medium severity, since the diff only touches README.md prose
Does not fabricate security, architecture, or code-quality findings against a documentation-only change
Still produces the full report structure rather than skipping sections
